displayName.text: Digital Battery Passport — Galaxy Lithium-ion Battery Cabinet (LIBSESMG17IEC)

description.text: Digital Battery Passport per IDTA 02035 (parts 1-7) for a Schneider Electric Galaxy Lithium-ion Battery Cabinet IEC with 17 x 2.04 kWh battery modules. EU Battery Regulation (EU) 2023/1542 POC. Data source: Battery Passport Data Attribute Longlist v1.2 sample values.
Contains the static nameplate attributes attached to the battery.

The battery passport identifier is the unique identifier of a battery passport.

description.text:
A battery passport must include information on the life cycle status of the battery.

The status of the battery must be defined as 'original' (0173-1#07-ACC020#001), 'repurposed'(0173-1#07-ACC021#001), 're-used'(0173-1#07-ACC022#001), 'remanufactured' (0173-1#07-ACC023#001) or 'waste' (0173-1#07-ACC024#001).

A new battery passport must be issued when a battery was subject to remanufacturing, repurpose or one of the treatment operations preparing for re-use and preparing for repurpose and is placed on the market again.


DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.3.7

* Carbon footprint label (6.2.4)

* Extinguishing agent (6.2.5)

* Meaning of labels and symbols (6.2.6)

Note: CE marking is declared as mandatory according to EU Blue Guide

description.text: The Submodel provides the means to access the Carbon Footprint of the asset.
Balance of greenhouse gas emissions along the entire life cycle of a product in a defined application and in relation to a defined unit of use.
Balance of greenhouse gas emissions along the entire life cycle of a product in a defined application and in relation to a defined unit of use.
Normen, Standards, Verfahren zur Ermittlung der Treibhausgas-Emissionen eines Produkts

Standards, methods for determining the greenhouse gas emissions of a product.

This Value List is based on ECLASS 15 with IRDI 0173-1#09-AAO115#003. The full list can be found in Section 2.5 in "Product Carbon Footprint 1.0 (IDTA-02023)".

value.value: TODO: confirm calculation methodology (source: PEP ENVPEP2104006_EN)

description.text: DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.2

If a normalized product carbon footprint (PCF) value is provided, the reference value for calculation should be a specific unit such as 'kWh'. 
If the total PCF value is provided the reference value for calculation should be 'piece'.

value.value: TODO: confirm reference impact unit (source data states kgCO2eq per functional unit)

description.text:
If a normalized product carbon footprint (PCF) value is provided, the quantity of measure for calculation should be 1.
If the total PCF value is provided the quantity of measure for calculation should be 1.

List of life cycle stages of the product according to the quantification requirements of the standard to which the PCF carbon footprint statement refers.

The string value in the value list is based on DIN DKE SPEC 99100: "A1 - raw material supply (and upstream production)" (6.3.5 required lify cycle mapping), "A3 - production" (6.3.4 Contribution of main product production/manufacturing lifecycle stage), "A4 - transport to final destination" (6.3.5 Contribution of distribution lifecycle stage), "C3 - recycling, waste treatment" (6.3.6 Contribution of end of life and recycling lifecycle stage)

Please note that the model should always contain at least one Product Carbon Footprint entry that reflects the total PCF value (including all life cycle phases) of the battery.

A battery passport must include information on the life cycle status of the battery.
   
   The status of the battery must be defined as 'original' (0173-1#07-ACC020#001), 'repurposed'(0173-1#07-ACC021#001), 're-used'(0173-1#07-ACC022#001), 'remanufactured' (0173-1#07-ACC023#001) or 'waste' (0173-1#07-ACC024#001).
   
   A new battery passport must be issued when a battery was subject to remanufacturing, repurpose or one of the treatment operations preparing for re-use and preparing for repurpose and is placed on the market again.
   
   DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.1.3.7

The unique operator identifier should comply with ISO/IEC 15459 1:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 2:2015, ISO/IEC 15459 3:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 4:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 5:2014, ISO/IEC 15459 6:2014, or their equivalent until referenced harmonised standards are listed in the OJEU.

description.text: This SubmodelElementCollection holds the information for a VDI 2770 Document entity

definition.text: Each SubmodelElementCollection describes a document by standard, which is associated to the particular Asset Administration Shell
Jede SubmodelElementCollection beschreibt ein Dokument (siehe IEC 82045-1 und IEC 8245-2), das der jeweiligen Asset Administration Shell zugeordnet ist
Set of document identifiers for the document. One ID in this collection should be used as a preferred ID
Satz von Dokument-IDs für das Dokument. Eine ID in dieser Sammlung sollte als bevorzugte ID verwendet werden
Information about a document identification entity
Information für eine Dokumentenidentifikations-Entität
Identification of the domain in which the given DocumentId is unique. The domain ID can e.g., be the name or acronym of the providing organisation
Identifikation der Domäne, in der die angegebene DocumentId eindeutig ist. Die Domain-ID kann z. B. der Name oder das Akronym der bereitstellenden Organisation sein
alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a document
alphanumerische Zeichenfolge, die ein Dokument eindeutig identifiziert

Flag indicating that a DocumentId within a collection of at least two DocumentId`s is the ‘primary’ identifier for the document. This is the preferred ID of the document (commonly from the point of view of the owner of the asset)

Flag, das angibt, dass eine DocumentId innerhalb einer Sammlung von mindestens zwei DocumentIds die „primäre“ Kennung für das Dokument ist. Dies ist die bevorzugte ID des Dokuments (üblicherweise aus Sicht des Eigentümers des Assets)

definition.text:
The battery passport must include the carbon footprint performance class in accordance with the entry into force of the delegated and implementing acts on the carbon footprint performance classes per Article7 of the Battery Regulation.

 DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.7

displayName.text: web link to public carbon footprint study

preferredName.text: web link to public carbon footprint study

definition.text:
One or more document identifiers (depending, for example, on the translation) of a specific document, which can be found in the 'HandoverDocumentation' model.

 DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.3.8
Alphanumerische Zeichenfolge, die ein Dokument eindeutig identifiziert.
Alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a document.
voltage the battery is rated for - NOM
voltage the battery is rated for - MAX
voltage the battery is rated for - MIN
total number of ampere-hours (Ah) that can be withdrawn from a fully charged battery under specific conditions
decrease over time and upon usage in the amount of charge that a battery can deliver at the rated voltage, with respect to the original rated capacity declared by the manufacturer
energy supplied by the battery from the beginning of the test procedure used for certification until the applicable break-off criterion of the test procedure used for certification is reached

initial round trip energy efficiency means the ratio of the net energy delivered by a battery during a discharge test to the total energy required to restore the initial State of Charge by a standard charge

displayName.text: round trip energy efficiency at 50 percent of cycle life

preferredName.text: round trip energy efficiency at 50% of cycle life

definition.text: round trip energy efficiency at 50% of cycle-life and measured at 50% of cycle life as determined in a pre-use standardized measurement
decrease of round trip energy efficiency as percentage, calculated from remaining and initial round trip energy efficiency
initial self-discharge in % of capacity per unit of time in defined conditions (temperature range etc) as pre-use metric

displayName.text: initial internal resistance at battery cell level

preferredName.text: initial internal resistance at battery cell level

definition.text: measure of a battery cell's opposition to current flow at the beginning of its operational life, affecting its performance, efficiency, and heat generation (internal resistance means the absolute value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse)

displayName.text: initial internal resistance at battery pack level

preferredName.text: initial internal resistance at battery pack level

definition.text: measure of opposition to current flow in an entire battery pack at the start of its operational life, affecting overall performance, efficiency, and heat generation (internal resistance means the absolute value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse)

displayName.text: initial internal resistance at battery module level

preferredName.text: initial internal resistance at battery module level

definition.text: initial internal resistance means the absolute beginning value of the quotient of the voltage drop between the terminals by the electric current increase in the battery circuit during a current pulse

displayName.text: internal resistance increase at battery pack level

preferredName.text: internal resistance increase at battery cell level

definition.text: increase of internal resistance in % as calculated from current and initial values (calculated from initial and current internal resistance on battery pack level)
maximum permitted power the battery is rated for, includes the data relevant for power limits
power capability at 80% and 20% state of charge (as defined in Battery Regulation Annex IV Part B)

List of pre-use power capabilities (definition of power capability as given in Battery Regulation).

Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which must include measurements at 80% and 20% state of charge for EV and industrial batteries.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.2

displayName.text: temperature range idle state lower boundary

definition.text: lower boundary of the surrounding temperature range, which the battery can safely withstand

displayName.text: temperature range idle state upper boundary

definition.text: upper boundary of the surrounding temperature range, which the battery can safely withstand

interpreted as minimum percentage of rated capacity, above which the battery is still considered operational as EV battery in its current life. The value has to be provided by the economic operator. This metric may serve as indicator for a necessary end of current life as EV and may be understood in the context of warranty.

The battery passport must include information about the period for which the commercial warranty applies.

DIN DKE Spec chapter reference: 6.1.3.4

Categories relevant for the battery passport: LMT battery, electric vehicle battery, stationary or other industrial battery >2kWh.

DIN DKE Spec chapter reference: 6.1.3.5

Mass of the entire battery in kilograms. Voluntary: if the battery is defined on pack or module level: also weight of the modules and/or cells.

DIN DKE Spec chapter reference: 6.1.3.6

displayName.text: internal resistance increase at battery module level

preferredName.text: internal resistance increase at battery module level

definition.text: Internal resistance increase at battery module level.

displayName.text: internal resistance increase at battery cell level

preferredName.text: internal resistance increase at battery cell level

definition.text: Internal resistance increase at battery cell level.
Power capability.
Power capability shall be measured at reference conditions, which must include measurements at 80% and 20% state of charge for EV and industrial batteries.
Individual battery characteristics based on DIN DKE SPEC 99100.

The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime in calendar years.

The update interval must be upon placement on the market and upon change of the battery status.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.2

Expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles.
The exception for non-cycle applications in Article 10 appears sensible, but is not included in the Annex XIII provision.
The data attribute is defined by measurement conditions of the cycle-life test such as the C-Rate (see below) and the depth of discharge in the cycle-life test

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.3

The nominal battery power is the suitable approximate value of the power capability used to designate or identify the battery, while the battery energy is determined in reference conditions to be defined.
   
   DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.6
General information, for example ordering and manufacturer information.

The C-rate should be provided separately for both the charge and discharge of the battery, if applicable.

The exception for non-cycle applications as mentioned in "Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles" should apply to this data attribute as well.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.6

displayName.text: reference test for 'Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles'

preferredName.text: reference test for 'Expected lifetime: Number of charge-discharge cycles'

definition.text:
The battery passport must include information about the expected battery lifetime expressed in cycles, and reference test used. 

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.5

description.text: Covers all battery lifetime relevant properties.

definition.text: Covers all battery lifetime relevant properties.

Overall sum of the energy throughput over the battery lifetime (data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by usable battery energy could add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes).

Timestamp for dynamic data attributes show the last update time.

Defined time within a time range, measured from the start time of the time range.

The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.7

Overall sum of the capacity throughput over the battery lifetime (data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity could add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes).

The data attribute should be reported as measured for further potential processing. In addition, the normalisation by capacity may add a further useful value that ensures comparability among battery sizes.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.8
Total count of times a battery has been fully charged from empty to full capacity and then fully discharged, used as an indicator of the battery's usage and lifespan.

Number of (full) charging and discharging cycles.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.6.4
Measured or on-board UBE performance at a specific point in its lifetime, expressed as a percentage of the certified usable battery energy.

Definition based on UNECE GTR 22: The measured or on-board UBE performance at a specific point in its lifetime, expressed as a percentage of the certified usable battery energy.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.7
UBE determined at the present point in the lifetime of the vehicle by the test procedure used for certification.

Definition from UNECE GTR 22, applicable only to EVs.
The energy supplied by the battery from the beginning of the test procedure used for certification until the applicable break-off criterion of the test procedure used for certification is reached.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.6
In-use data attribute on capacity, corresponding with the definition of rated capacity.

The in-use data attribute on capacity, corresponding with the definition of rated capacity.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.2.3
Negative event, such as an accident. No further definition provided by regulation.

The battery passport must contain information and data resulting from its use such as accidents. 

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.8.4

The battery passport must contain information and data resulting from its use such as accidents. 

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.8.4
RPC last updated.
Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.
Power capability at.
Remaining power capability value.

Original power capability (in Watts) and limits, with temperature range when relevant.
- The amount of energy that a battery is capable to provide over a given period of time under reference conditions.
- Power capability at 80% and 20% state of charge.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.3.3
Total count of times a battery has been fully charged from empty to full capacity and then fully discharged, used as an indicator of the battery's usage and lifespan.

Evolution of self-discharge rates is the change of self-discharge over time and usage, as percentage calculated from the initial and current self-discharge rate.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.8
Current self-discharge in % of capacity per unit of time in defined conditions (temperature range) during the use phase.

The current self-discharge rate is the change of the self-discharge rate in an idle state of the battery in reference conditions (temperature etc.) at aging parameter x, e.g. after a certain amount of storage time or, number of cycles.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.7

displayName.text: remaining round trip energy efficiency value

preferredName.text: remaining round trip energy efficiency value

definition.text: Percentage of energy that can be recovered and used after a full charge and discharge cycle, reflecting the battery's ability to retain and return energy efficiently over time.

The battery passport shall include information, where possible, about the remaining round trip energy efficiency as information on the state of health of the battery 

The update frequency of remaining round trip energy efficiency should be aligned with the update frequency of round trip energy efficiency fade and should be provided upon change of the battery status.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.4.4
Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.
Alphanumeric character sequence uniquely identifying a document.
Alphanumerische Zeichenfolge, die ein Dokument eindeutig identifiziert.
Defined by 'pre-use power capability (definition of power capability as given in Battery Regulation.

The battery passport must include periodically recorded information on the operating environmental conditions, including temperature.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.7.7.5 - 8
Cumulated time spent below the given lower boundary of temperature during charging.
Cumulated time spent above the given upper boundary of temperature.
Cumulated time spent above the given upper boundary of temperature during Charging.
Cumulated time spent below the given lower boundary of temperature.
Available capacity in a battery expressed as a percentage of remaining capacity.

description.text:
Mandatory data: Battery chemistry; critical raw materials; materials used in the cathode, anode, and 
electrolyte; hazardous substances; impact of substances on the environment and on human health or 
safety.

definition.text:
Mandatory data: Battery chemistry; critical raw materials; materials used in the cathode, anode, and 
electrolyte; hazardous substances; impact of substances on the environment and on human health or 
safety.
Short name.
Clear name.
Impact.

Composition of a product in general terms by specifying the cathode and anode active material as well as electrolyte.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.2
Name of component.
Identifier of the component.
Battery component that relates to the material.

Registration number of an international designation standard (CAS = Chemical Abstracts Service) for chemical substances. For each chemical substance registered in the CAS database (also biosequences, alloys, polymers) there is a unique CAS number.

Clear name of Material.

Alphanumeric character string which describes the object.
Weight of component material.

The battery passport must contain information on the critical raw materials present in the battery.

The information on the critical raw materials must also be provided on the battery label.
Per Annex VI, Part A(10), critical raw materials must be reported if present in the battery in a concentration of more than 0,1 % weight by weight.
Material of battery.

"Component materials used" (No. 17.a-c): Naming the materials (as a composition of substances) in cathode, anode, electrolyte according to public standards, including specification of the corresponding component (i.e., cathode, anode, or electrolyte). We suggest a reporting threshold of 0.1 % weight by weight.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.3-6.5.4

"Hazardous substance" (No 20.a-e): Name (agreed substance nomenclature, e.g. IUPAC or chemical name) a hazardous substance (as 'any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment'). Suggested above 0.1 % weight by weight within each (sub-)component.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.4 - 6.5.6
Battery Regulation narrows reporting to substances falling under defined hazard classes and categories of the CLP regulation.
Clear name of hazardous substance.
Concentration of hazardous substance.
Impact statements based on, e.g., REACH or GHS for all hazard classes applicable to substances in the battery.

Hazardous substances (No. 19-23): Location on a (sub-)component-level of all hazardous substances (as 'any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment'). Suggested via a unique identifier or nomenclature.
CAS identifier of hazardous substance.

"Hazardous substances" (No 20.a-e): Name (agreed substance nomenclature, e.g. IUPAC or chemical name) all hazardous substance (as 'any substance that poses a threat to human health and the environment'). Suggested above 0.1 % weight by weight within each (sub-)component.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.5.4 - 6.5.6

description.text:
Dismantling information (including at least: exploded diagrams of the battery system/pack showing the location of battery cells; disassembly sequences; type and number of fastening techniques to be unlocked; tools required for disassembly; warnings if risk of damaging parts exists; amount of cells used and layout); part numbers for components and contact details of sources for replacement spares; safety measures 
   
   (Annex XIII (2b-d)); usable extinguishing agent (Annex VI, Part A(9)).

definition.text:
Dismantling information (including at least: exploded diagrams of the battery system/pack showing the location of battery cells; disassembly sequences; type and number of fastening techniques to be unlocked; tools required for disassembly; warnings if risk of damaging parts exists; amount of cells used and layout); part numbers for components and contact details of sources for replacement spares; safety measures 
   
   (Annex XIII (2b-d)); usable extinguishing agent (Annex VI, Part A(9)).

Share of renewable material content. A renewable material is a material made of natural resources that can be replenished. 

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.2.11

Dismantling and Removal information, including at least:
   * Exploded diagrams of the battery system/pack showing the location of battery cells
   * Disassembly sequences
   * Type and number of fastening techniques to be unlocked
   * Tools required for disassembly
   * Warnings if risk of damaging parts exists
   * Amount of cells used and layout. 
   
   BR Annex XIII (2c)

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.2
   
    > NOTE: For more information on the document identified by its document ID please refer to the Handover Documentation of the battery.
Name of the company.
Länderkürzel.
Code of a country (Country codes defined accord. to DIN EN ISO 3166-1).
postal code of address.
Straße und Nummer.
Street name and house number.
Postal address of supplier for spare parts.
Electronic mail address of a business partner.
Public part of an unsymmetrical key pair to sign or encrypt text or messages.

Characterization of an e-mail address according to its location or usage
enumeration: 0173-1#07-AAS754#001 (office), 0173-1#07-AAS756#001 (secretary), 0173-1#07-AAS757#001 (substitute), 0173-1#07-AAS758#001 (home).
Characterization of a public key according to its encryption process.
E-mail address and encryption method.
Web site address where information about the product or contact is given.
Name of the part.
Part number of Component.
Components available at supplier.

Contact details of sources for replacement spares. Postal address, including name and brand names, postal code and place, street and number, country, telephone, if any. BR Annex XIII (2b)

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.3
The part numbers for components should be provided together with the postal address, e-mail address and web address of the sources for spare parts. \n\nDIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.3
Recycled material share from pre-consumer waste (manufacturing waste, excluding run-around scrap) of the active material.
Name of recycled material
Recycled material share from post-consumer waste (end-of-life scrap) of the active material.

A battery passport must include recycled content information.\n\nThe content information must include the percentage share of nickel that is present in active materials and that has been recovered from battery manufacturing waste, for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

Share of material recovered from waste present in active materials for each battery model per year and per manufacturing plant.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference:  6.6.2.3 - 6.6.2.10
Necessary safety instructions to handle waste batteries, including in relation to the risks associated with, and the handling of, batteries containing lithium.
Usable extinguishing agents refering to classes of extinguishers (A, B, C, D, K). EUBR: Annex XIII (1a), Annex VI Part A (9)

Safety measures and instructions should also take past negative and extreme events as well as the separate data attributes 'battery status' and 'battery composition/chemistry' into account.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.1.5

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (a) of Article 60(1): Information on the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention, including by information on good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aiming at extending their use phase and the possibilities of re-use, preparing for re-use, preparing for repurpose, repurposing and remanufacturing

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (b) of Article 60(1): Information on the role of end-users in contributing to the separate collection of waste batteries in accordance with their obligations under Article 51 so as to allow their treatment

Producer or producer responsibility organisations shall make information available to distributors and end-users on: the role of end-users in contributing to waste prevention, including by information on good practices and recommendations concerning the use of batteries aiming at extending their use phase and the possibilities of re-use, preparing for re-use, preparing for repurpose, repurposing and remanufacturing.

DIN DKE Spec 99100 chapter reference: 6.6.3.2 - 6.6.3.4
Extinguishing agent.

Prevention and management of waste batteries: Point (c) of Article 60(1): information on the separate collection, the take back, the collection points and preparing for re-use, preparing for repurposing, and recycling operations available for waste batteries
